description: A cup slips from a hand and moves downwards once its support is gone. That gravity-led descent is 'lepa', as are falling rain and leaves. 'tupi' (jump) includes the launch from a surface; 'lepa' begins with the unsupported movement.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for voiced lateral 'l', let the voice pass around its sides, and move into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p', release them, and finish with open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The lateral opening lets the voice travel freely before the lips close at 'p'. Their release into broad final 'a' gives the short word a clear landing.
usage_notes: Causative 'ka' adds the agent that makes another thing fall. 'muo' (INTO) marks what the subject falls into, while 'mua' (LOC) locates a descent already happening within a place or medium.

semantic_domains.activity: an event in which a body loses support and descends
semantic_domains.physical: gravity-led movement after support is lost
semantic_domains.spatial: a path from a higher position to a lower one
